questions.explanation: شو (shu) is the Lebanese way to say 'what'. مين (meen) = who, إيمتى (ēmta) = when, كيف (kīf) = how.

questions.prompt: How do you say "I don't know" in Lebanese Arabic?
questions.explanation: ما بعرف (ma baʕrif) uses the Lebanese negation 'ma' + b-prefix verb. لا أعرف is MSA. ما بدي = I don't want. مش منيح = not good.

options: Someone is eating or as a toast
questions.explanation: صحتين (saḥtēn) literally means 'two healths' — it's the Lebanese 'bon appétit' or 'cheers', said when someone is eating or drinking.

questions.prompt: The Lebanese present tense uses which prefix on verbs?
questions.explanation: The بـ (b-) prefix marks habitual/present tense in Lebanese Arabic. سـ is MSA future. This b-prefix doesn't exist in MSA — it's distinctly Lebanese.
The suffix ـي (-i) means 'my'. بيتك = your house, بيتو = his house, بيتنا = our house.
أخضر (akhḍar) means green. أحمر (aḥmar) = red, أزرق (azra') = blue, أصفر (aṣfar) = yellow. Lebanese colors follow the same root patterns as MSA but with dialectal pronunciation.

questions.prompt: How do you say "Monday" in Lebanese Arabic?
questions.explanation: الاثنين (il-tinēn) is Monday. Note the Lebanese pronunciation differs from MSA الاثنين (al-ithnayn). الأحد = Sunday, التلاتا = Tuesday, الأربعا = Wednesday.
راسي (rāsi) means 'my head'. إيدي (īdi) = my hand, إجري (ijri) = my foot, ضهري (dahri) = my back. The ـي suffix marks possession ('my').
تفاحة (tiffēḥa) means apple. موزة (mawze) = banana, ليمونة (laymūne) = lemon/orange, عنب (ʕinab) = grapes. Lebanese fruit names are used daily at the سوق (sūʔ, market).

questions.prompt: How do you say "my mother" in Lebanese Arabic?
questions.explanation: أمي (immi) means 'my mother'. أبي (bayyi) = my father, أختي (ikhti) = my sister, ستي (sitti) = my grandmother. Note: Lebanese uses bayyi for father, not the MSA أبي (abī).
صباح الخير (ṣabāḥ il-khēr) means 'good morning'. The response is صباح النور (ṣabāḥ in-nūr, 'morning of light'). مساء الخير = good evening, تصبح على خير = good night, مرحبا = hello.
خبز (khubiz) means bread — a staple of Lebanese cuisine. رز (rizz) = rice, جبنة (jibne) = cheese, لحمة (laḥme) = meat. Bread is central to every Lebanese meal.
كيفك (kīfak, to male / kīfik, to female) means 'how are you?' — one of the most common Lebanese greetings. من وين إنت = where are you from, شو إسمك = what is your name, قديش عمرك = how old are you.

title: Phase 1 Fill in the Blank — Basic Vocabulary

fillBlanks.sentence: The word ___ means "hello" in Lebanese Arabic.
fillBlanks.hint: It starts with 'm' and is used as a general greeting.

fillBlanks.sentence: To say "thank you" in Lebanese, you say ___.
fillBlanks.hint: Same as in MSA — starts with 'sh'.
Short two-letter word, not the MSA ماء.
The greeting you use when you wake up.

fillBlanks.sentence: In Lebanese Arabic, "my name is" translates to ___ إسمي.

fillBlanks.hint: A short word — not the MSA نعم.

fillBlanks.sentence: The Lebanese word for "good" or "fine" is ___.
fillBlanks.hint: Used to say you are doing well — كيفك؟ ___!

matchingPairs.english: God keep you safe (response to goodbye)
May God give you strength (said to someone working)
